differences.title: 1. DATA MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Structured data in predefined schemas, ensuring integrity.
differences.rightDesc: Unstructured data in JSON-like documents, allowing schema flexibility.

differences.title: 2. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: More powerful hardware for improved performance.
differences.rightDesc: More servers for distributed load and capacity.

differences.title: 3. COMPLEX QUERIES
differences.leftDesc: Supports complex joins, aggregations, and transactions efficiently.
differences.rightDesc: Designed for document retrieval, less suited for complex joins.

differences.title: 4. ACID COMPLIANCE
differences.leftDesc: Guarantees Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability for transactions.
differences.rightDesc: Prioritizes availability, data becomes consistent over time.

differences.title: 5. SCHEMA MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Schema defined upfront, changes require migrations.
differences.rightDesc: Schema can evolve with data, easier iteration.

differences.title: 6. RELATIONSHIPS
differences.rightDesc: Related data often embedded within a single document.

differences.title: 7. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for financial systems, complex analytics, and legacy apps.
differences.rightDesc: Great for blogs, catalogs, user profiles, and big data.
